topic.title: Oxygen XML Desktop - Security and Data Integrity

body.p: We receive sometimes questions about how secure the Oxygen XML Editor/Author/Developer desktop applications are, and in this blog post I will try to discuss various security and data integrity aspects.
With Oxygen's layers of security and your vigilance, you will have safe and productive editing sessions with our tools.

section.title: Certifications
section.p: We do not do penetration tests of type SOC2 for Oxygen desktop as this kind of tests are for server applications. Oxygen desktop is installed on each user's platform and users have full control over the content they edit or outside resources accessible to the application.

section.title: Unsafe Libraries
section.p:
Oxygen contains a variety of open source and commercial libraries. They are all listed and endorsed in the main menu Help About dialog. In time various Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) may be reported for certain libraries used by the application or by plugins we built for it. We monitor such situations in real time and depending on how the CVEs affect the application we may decide to:
Release as soon as possible new versions of the applications or plugins with the libraries updated. We only take this decision when the risks are severe.
Consider the CVEs do not affect our application as it does not use the vulnerable parts of the libraries. In such case we issue security advisories and possibly update the libraries in future releases.

section.title: Privacy
section.p: By default Oxygen may connect to its web site to retrieve information about various events (new releases, conferences, webinars). You can decide to disable these notification checks.
By default Oxygen also allows you to install add-ons from its default add-ons update site and the privacy settings above can also control this setting.

section.title: Add-ons - Plugins
section.p: You can install plugins in Oxygen via the add-ons installation support. You will receive the EULA of the add-on and also information about if the add-on is signed or not. Oxygen has a bunch of useful official add-ons which can be installed. Do not install third party add-on if they come from unverified or untrusted sources. Oxygen plugins have access rights similar to Oxygen so they can make modifications on your computer or even run other processes.
If an installed plugin makes Oxygen un-responsive in certain circumstances you can un-install it from the Oxygen Preferences Plugins page and contact its vendors.
Plugins may add side views in Oxygen allowing you to open and save documents from external locations, do not use these capabilities if you do not trust the plugin or the external storage system.

section.title: Add-ons - Frameworks
section.p: You can install frameworks in Oxygen via the add-ons installation support. Frameworks allow you to add specific editing support for certain XML files. When you perform the installation you receive the EULA of the add-on and also information about if the add-on is signed or not. Do not install third party frameworks if they come from unverified or untrusted sources. Java extensions in Oxygen frameworks have access rights similar to Oxygen so they can make modifications on your computer or even run other processes.
If an installed framework makes Oxygen un-responsive in certain circumstances you can un-install it from the Oxygen Preferences Document Type Association page and contact its vendors.

section.title: Connections to Remote Servers
section.p: Whenever Oxygen's functionality attempts to open an HTTP or HTTPS connection to a remote server you will get a popup dialog asking you if the connection should be allowed. You can configure your trusted hosts in the Trusted Hosts Preferences page. Connections to HTTP resources can either retrieve unapproved content or they can send content to the remote server, so you must reject connections to web site that you consider unsafe.

section.title: Project Level Settings
section.p: Most of Oxygen's Preferences pages can be saved at Project level and shared with your team. When you receive an Oxygen project XPR file having custom settings and attempt to open it in Oxygen, you will receive a notification the XPR has custom settings inside. Do not approve loading of custom settings from an XPR received from an untrusted source.

section.title: Unsafe Content
section.p: You may receive XML documents and other documents (for example XSLT stylesheets) from other people. Do not open in general documents received from untrusted sources. Oxygen implements ample mechanisms to avoid various public security vulnerabilities related to XML processing. When processing or editing content received from untrusted sources, if Oxygen shows a dialog asking you to connect to an external web site, consider if the web site is trusted.

section.title: Crashes - Data Integrity
section.p: Oxygen attempts to secure the content you are editing (and have not yet saved) in the unlikely event of an application crash. In the Oxygen Preferences Editor / Save page by default you have settings like Save save (which saves the entire content of a file instead of just streaming the modifications) or Save auto-recover information enabled.
If the application crashes while you are editing a document and then gets restarted it should allow you to recover the contents of the edited document.

section.title: Out of Memory
section.p: The entire Oxygen application is allocated a maximum amount of memory when started. You can see the maximum amount of internal memory allocated to Oxygen in the main menu Help About dialog. You can also decide to increase the maximum amount of memory allocated to Oxygen.

The application has a set of features which allow it to avoid out of memory situations:
Optimizations and restrictions when loading very large or huge files.
Lazily loading the last opened files when a project is opened in the Project view. Only the tabs of the current selected files are loaded and all other files are loaded when pressing their corresponding tabs.
Starting with Oxygen 29, when there is little memory available the application shows a warning and starts unloading opened documents, leaving only their tabs. The documents are loaded back when you press their corresponding tab.

section.title: Stay in Touch
section.p: Active licenses of Oxygen desktop (licenses which are not in our end of life stage) allow you to contact us via our many channels and ask for advice or help related to various unstable or unsafe behaviors of the application. You can also use the Oxygen main menu Help Report problem dialog to report such problems to us.
